nodes.body: <<FILL role=setup words=135 beats='Wren, the youngest apprentice at the Chart House, is rowed to a nameless island at first light and given one task, fill the blank paper before the ship returns at dusk or lose the apprenticeship; the beach opens onto five ways inland, the tangled mangrove coast, the green cloud-forest ridge, the dark sink-hole caverns, a leaning ruined survey tower, and the wide shining tidal flats'>>

choices.label: Wade into the mangrove coast.
Climb toward the cloud-forest ridge.
Descend into the sink-hole caverns.
Cross to the ruined survey tower.
Walk out onto the tidal flats.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=110 beats='the mangrove coast is a maze of roots and brown water where herons stand still as sticks; Wren must pick a bearing before the mud swallows the morning, upcoast toward a fallen log bridge, downcoast toward a cluster of oyster rocks, or straight inland along a hidden creek'>>

choices.label: Follow the log bridge upcoast.
Pick along the oyster rocks downcoast.
Push inland up the hidden creek.
Wade toward the still green lagoon.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the fallen log is slick and green, crossing a channel where the tide is already turning; halfway over Wren spots a knot of driftwood carved with old marks and a heron rookery loud in the branches on the far bank'>>

choices.label: Stop to copy the carved driftwood.
Hurry across before the tide.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='the driftwood carries a mapmaker's shorthand, arrows and a little compass rose scratched by someone long ago; the marks point two ways, back toward the sea caves and up toward high ground'>>

choices.label: Trust the arrow toward high ground.
Read the marks slowly for a message.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=100 beats='read slowly, the shorthand is a message, the last mapmaker signing a name, Alder, and a promise to leave a full chart at the ridge camp; the arrow up the hill is clear and sure'>>

choices.label: Head for high ground as Alder marked.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='on the far bank the rookery erupts, hundreds of herons lifting at once, and in the racket Wren nearly misses a narrow path splitting left toward a mud slick and right toward a stand of firm palms'>>

choices.label: Cross the mud slick quickly.
Keep to the firm palms.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='the mud slick is deeper than it looks and grips like glue up to the knee; by the time Wren wriggles free the tide has closed the log bridge behind, so this whole finger of coast is cut off and stays a blank corner on the paper, and Wren backs out to firmer ground for the day'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the ground lifts out of the swamp into firm palms, and from a low rise Wren can see the whole northern shape of the island, a curl of reef and a bright notch that must be a hidden cove; a goat track leads on toward it'>>

choices.label: Take the goat track down to the cove.
Angle inland toward a spring gully.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the oyster rocks are sharp and busy with crabs, and among them Wren finds a shape that does not belong, a small sea turtle turned on its back in a tide pool, flippers waving, stranded by the falling water'>>

choices.label: Study the turtle and read the tide pools.
Search the rocks for a way inland.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='the turtle is stuck but not hurt; Wren could right it now and lose precious time, or mark its exact spot and the reef channel on the map and send help back for it once a high vantage is found'>>

choices.label: Mark the turtle and press on inland.
Stop and right the turtle now.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=95 beats='Wren tips the turtle upright and it scuttles to the water, but the delay lets the tide flood the rocks, and with wet paper and slipping light Wren maps only this one corner, a kind deed done and an honest sketch of the reef, while the rest of the island stays blank'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='past the rocks a seep of clear water threads inland through the salt marsh, colder and sweeter the higher it climbs, the sure sign of a spring somewhere above the swamp'>>

choices.label: Follow the clear seep upward.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the seep climbs out of the marsh into a shaded gully of ferns; the water is properly cold now and the gully forks, one arm toward a bright pool that must be the spring, one arm toward a mossy overhang hung with vines'>>

choices.label: Climb to the bright spring pool.
Duck under the mossy overhang.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=100 beats='under the overhang the rock is dry and someone has sheltered here, an old firepit and a dented tin cup, and scratched on the stone a single word, Alder, with an arrow pointing uphill toward a camp'>>

choices.label: Follow Alder's arrow toward the camp.
Copy the firepit shelter and rest a while.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='the dry shelter is so welcome after the swamp that Wren sits to sketch it and the morning slides by unfelt; when the light shifts Wren jumps up with only this one snug corner drawn, and hurries out knowing far too much of the island is still bare paper'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the hidden creek runs inland straight and slow under a green roof, so quiet Wren can hear paddling, and around a bend a mother otter and two kits watch from a half sunk log before slipping away upstream'>>

choices.label: Follow the otters upstream.
Cut away from the water into the trees.
Pole across to the loud heron island.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='the otters lead to a clean gravel bar where the creek shallows, and beyond it the ground rises firm and dry; the water here is sweet, fed from somewhere higher up the island'>>

choices.label: Climb along the sweet water.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='away from the creek the trees close in and the light goes green and dim; without the water to follow Wren must pick a direction by instinct, uphill toward a hint of brightness or along a level game trail'>>

choices.label: Climb toward the brightness.
Take the level game trail.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='the game trail runs level and easy but keeps curling back on itself, and Wren realises with a jolt that the mangrove has no true landmark here, only more of itself in every direction'>>

choices.label: Backtrack toward the bright rise.
Gamble on one more bend.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='the last bend gives onto the same fallen tree Wren passed an hour ago; the mangrove has been walking Wren in a slow circle, and with the sun already high the coast is drawn as a rough guess, honest but unfinished, and Wren turns back to try firmer ground'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=110 beats='the cloud-forest ridge climbs straight off the beach into cool mist, dripping moss and birdsong; a well worn switchback goes up sharply, a gentler traverse angles across the slope, and a side path drops to a mossy hollow where something pale gleams'>>

choices.label: Take the steep switchback up.
Angle across the gentle traverse.
Drop to the pale gleam in the hollow.
Follow the roar toward a hidden waterfall.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the switchback is steep but honest, each turn opening a wider view of the island below; Wren pauses on a flat ledge where two ways continue, a bare rock rib straight up and a green shelf that traces toward a break in the trees'>>

choices.label: Climb the bare rock rib.
Follow the green shelf.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the rock rib is airy and exposed, cloud streaming past, but it gains height fast and delivers Wren onto a broad grassy bench where the ridge proper begins and a worn path runs on toward higher ground'>>

choices.label: Follow the ridge path higher.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='the green shelf traces a contour line, exactly the sort of level path a surveyor would cut, and it curves patiently around the slope to the same grassy bench where the true ridge path begins'>>

choices.label: Join the ridge path.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the grassy bench is the ridge's shoulder, wind combing the tussocks; from here a stony trail climbs toward the high camp Wren keeps hearing about, while off to the side a hush of giant mossy trees marks an ancient grove'>>

choices.label: Climb the stony trail toward the camp.
Detour into the grove of ancient trees.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='the last stretch toward the camp is a scramble over broken rock where the path almost disappears, but a line of small cairns, set by careful hands, points the safe way up onto the high ridge'>>

choices.label: Follow the cairns onto the high ridge.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='up on the open ridge the wind is a live thing and the world falls away on both sides; the cairns march on ahead toward the far grey speck of the camp, over ground that rises in long tired steps of rock and heather'>>

choices.label: Push on along the cairned ridge.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='the ridge splits around a shoulder of rock, the cairns going left along a sheltered ledge out of the wind, while a bolder line goes right straight over the airy crest with the whole island below'>>

choices.label: Take the sheltered ledge.
Brave the airy crest.
Stop here, daunted by the height.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='the drop on both sides makes Wren's head swim, and after a long frozen minute the only honest thing is to sit down and edge back the way that felt safe; the camp stays a grey speck out of reach, and Wren maps the ridge from below, the high chart left for a braver day'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='the sheltered ledge is slow but sure, out of the worst of the wind, threading below the crest past cushions of tiny alpine flowers until it climbs back to the ridgeline where the paths rejoin'>>

choices.label: Rejoin the ridgeline.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='the airy crest is pure exhilaration, a walk along the very spine of the island with cloud pouring over from the far side, and it delivers Wren, breathless and grinning, back to where the cairns gather at a level shelf'>>

choices.label: Come down to the level shelf.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='the two ways rejoin at a broad level shelf where the last cairns cluster thick, and a final short pull over grass and rock leads up toward the cliff where the camp must stand'>>

choices.label: Make the final pull to the camp.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='and there it is, tucked at last against the cliff, the grey canvas roof and low stone walls of Alder's ridge camp, exactly where every clue on the island has been pointing all day long'>>

choices.label: Step into Alder's camp.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the traverse is gentle but long, crossing streams that all run the same way downhill; following one upstream Wren reaches a clear cold pool ringed with ferns, plainly the island's spring'>>

choices.label: Rest at the spring.
Keep traversing along the slope.
Follow a deer path to a hidden dell.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='higher along the traverse the mist thins and a stand of wind bent trees gives way to open ground; a faint path climbs on, and below it a mossy hollow shows the same pale gleam Wren glimpsed from the beach'>>

choices.label: Climb the faint path on.
Drop to the pale gleam.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=100 beats='the pale gleam is a curtain of white orchids over a rock face, and behind them a dry cleft where a tin box has been wedged, green with age and marked with a compass rose'>>

choices.label: Open the tin box.
Leave the box and climb back up.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='inside the box are folded papers, a stub of pencil, and a brass locket; the papers are field notes in a careful hand, and the locket holds a faded picture of a girl who looks a little like Wren'>>

choices.label: Read the field notes.
Pocket the locket and hurry on.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=95 beats='Wren keeps the locket and climbs on, but spends the rest of the morning wondering about the girl in the picture instead of watching the land, and the ridge is mapped in a distracted scrawl, a small mystery carried home in place of a finished chart'>>

<<FILL role=discovery words=100 beats='the notes are Alder's, the mapmaker who came before, describing a full chart hidden at the ridge camp and a warning that the caverns flood at high tide; the last line points uphill to the camp'>>

choices.label: Follow Alder's notes toward the camp.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=115 beats='a cool breath of air rises from a sink-hole ringed with ferns, and a knotted rope left by someone earlier dangles into the dark; below, the shaft opens into a cavern with three passages, one echoing with dripping water, one glittering faintly, and one breathing warm air from deeper in'>>

choices.label: Follow the dripping water.
Head toward the glitter.
Trace the warm breathing air.
Climb toward a high hole of daylight.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the dripping passage slopes down to an underground stream, clear and cold, running fast toward a low arch; a line scratched high on the wall warns exactly where the water rises at the turn of the tide'>>

choices.label: Wade the stream toward the arch.
Climb the stream upward against the flow.
Explore a side rift lit with glowworms.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='the arch is low and the water is rising; a dry ledge runs beside it toward a paler light, but pressing under the arch itself would be a shortcut into the dark heart of the caves'>>

choices.label: Edge along the dry ledge to the light.
Duck under the low arch.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='past the arch the roof drops to a hand's width above the water, and as Wren hesitates the tide pushes in and the passage begins to fill; there is just time to scramble back, soaked and shaking, the caverns left as a single dark question mark on the map'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='climbing against the current, the stream grows sweeter and the roof lifts into a bright chamber where a shaft of daylight falls on a pool, the underground face of the island's spring'>>

choices.label: Climb the daylight shaft to the spring.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=105 beats='the glitter is not treasure but crystal, veins of quartz webbing the walls, and pressed among them handprints in red ochre, older than any chart, someone marking this place long before mapmakers came'>>

choices.label: Copy the handprints carefully.
Push past toward a further glow.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=95 beats='copying the handprints, Wren sees they make a rough shape, the island itself, with a mark where the spring is and a mark at the high peak, a map drawn by the first people to find this place'>>

choices.label: Follow the ochre mark toward the spring.
Fill a whole page with the old painting.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=95 beats='Wren fills a whole page with the ochre handprints and the island shape around them, a record no chart ever held; it costs the afternoon, and the land above stays half drawn, but the Chart House has never seen such proof that people lived here long before any surveyor'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the further glow is daylight leaking through a crack, and squeezing toward it Wren tumbles out blinking onto a hidden shelf of beach inside a ring of cliffs, the hidden cove seen from below; back the way came, a side passage murmurs with lapping water'>>

choices.label: Climb up into the cove.
Follow the passage toward the lapping water.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the warm passage breathes like a sleeping animal, and around a corner Wren finds why, a colony of little bats and, curled on a ledge below them, a young seal pup that has wriggled in through some sea entrance and cannot climb back out'>>

choices.label: Study how the pup got in.
Back away and leave the warm dark.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='the pup came through a flooded tube from the sea and is stranded by the low tide; Wren cannot lift it alone, but marking its spot and finding a high place to signal the ship might bring help before dusk'>>

choices.label: Mark the pup and climb toward the light.
Try to coax the pup out alone now.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='the pup is heavier than it looks and frightened, and after a long slippery struggle Wren has to admit it needs more than one pair of hands; Wren marks the sea tube on the map and backs out, the rescue left for the tide and better luck'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='backing out of the warm dark, Wren finds a side vent choked with roots and, beyond it, a slot of daylight opening onto firm ground high on the slope'>>

choices.label: Squeeze out toward the daylight.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='Wren climbs toward the light and air, following the bats as they stream out at a crack, and comes up onto the open flank of the ridge with the whole island tilting away below and the camp's grey canvas not far above'>>

choices.label: Make for the ridge camp.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=110 beats='the survey tower leans on a headland, half its stone stair fallen, a place built by mapmakers before Wren was born; the door hangs open on a ground room, an outside stair spirals up toward the broken top, and a cellar hatch is set in the floor'>>

choices.label: Search the ground room.
Climb the outside stair.
Lift the cellar hatch.
Explore the keeper's side quarters.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=100 beats='the ground room is full of ruined instruments, a rusted theodolite, rotten ropes, and a slate desk where a logbook lies swollen with damp, its last pages still just readable'>>

choices.label: Read the swollen logbook.
Salvage a working instrument.
Pry up a loose slab in the floor.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=95 beats='the logbook is Alder's, and its last entry says the true chart was carried to the ridge camp for safekeeping before a storm; Wren copies the words but the light is going, and carries home a story of the lost mapmaker instead of a finished map'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='one instrument still turns, a battered alidade for sighting distant points; with it Wren could fix the island's shape fast from a high place, if a high place can be reached in time'>>

choices.label: Take the alidade toward the ridge camp.
Take it up the tower stair first.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='carrying the alidade like a treasure, Wren follows the headland path inland where it climbs to meet the main ridge, the grey canvas of the camp showing far ahead against the cliff'>>

choices.label: Follow the headland path to the ridge.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the outside stair is missing steps and open to a long drop to the rocks; two thirds up, a gap forces a choice, a bold stride across to the next slab or a slow clamber along a crumbling ledge'>>

choices.label: Stride boldly across the gap.
Clamber the crumbling ledge.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='the crumbling ledge sheds stones under Wren's boots and there is nothing to do but retreat, heart pounding, back to solid ground; the tower keeps its high view to itself, and that corner of the island is drawn from guesswork below'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=105 beats='from the tower top the island shows its bones, the ridge, the reef, the pale flats, and a bright ring of cliffs hiding a cove; a surveyor could take rough bearings here, but the true summit stands higher still, reached only across the ridge by way of the camp'>>

choices.label: Cross the ridge toward the camp.
Climb down toward the hidden cove.
Examine the old beacon brazier.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='the way from the tower to the ridge runs along a knife of rock with the sea on both sides, thrilling and slow, until it joins the broad grassy bench where the camp trail begins'>>

choices.label: Join the camp trail.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=100 beats='the cellar is dry and cool and stacked with the surveyors' stores, rope, lamp oil, tins, and rolled against the wall a leather tube that might hold a chart'>>

choices.label: Open the leather tube.
Take rope and oil and climb back out.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='the tube holds a chart, but it is half finished and water stained, Alder's abandoned draft, with a note that the good copy went to the ridge camp; it shows enough to guess the safe path up'>>

choices.label: Follow the draft toward the ridge camp.
Sit and copy the ruined draft in full.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=90 beats='Wren spends the morning reading Alder's ruined draft and copying what survives, and it is real work, but it is Alder's map, not Wren's; the day ends with a faithful copy of someone else's unfinished island and no new ground of Wren's own'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='with rope and oil coiled over a shoulder Wren is ready for cliff or cave, and a surveyor's chalk line points from the cellar door straight along a marked path toward the high ground of the ridge'>>

choices.label: Follow the chalk line to the ridge.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=110 beats='the tidal flats stretch wide and mirror bright, firm sand near shore turning to soft ooze far out where the sea has drawn back; the flats hide channels and a distant sandbar where birds crowd, and the water will return, everyone knows, faster than a person can run'>>

choices.label: Wade toward the distant sandbar.
Trace the firm channel edges.
Search the strand line near shore.
Pick across the dark mussel beds.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='the sandbar is a treasure of wreck timber and a whole boat's ribs, and it would map beautifully, but the sand underfoot is already darkening as the tide turns far out; Wren has minutes, not hours'>>

choices.label: Sketch fast, then run for shore.
Linger over the wreck too long.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='the sketch is good and the run is not fast enough; the tide fills the low channels first and Wren wades back chest deep, chart safe overhead but soaked, and spends what is left of the day drying out on shore, the flats a bright blank on the paper'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='Wren sketches the wreck in furious strokes and runs, reaching firm sand as the first sheet of water slides across behind; heart hammering, but the flats are drawn and a dry way inland opens toward a green gap in the shore'>>

choices.label: Follow the green gap inland.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='the green gap is the mouth of a valley where a stream comes down to meet the sea, and following it up on firm banks Wren climbs toward the sound of falling water and cool air'>>

choices.label: Climb the stream toward the falls.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the firm channel edges make a natural road across the flats, and Wren follows them safely toward shore where they gather at the foot of the ring of cliffs, a gap of dark wet sand leading in'>>

choices.label: Follow the channels to the cliff gap.
Cut inland up a firm rise.
Detour to the great wreck on the bar.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='the cliff gap is a slot of wet sand that the sea only leaves bare at low water, and through it Wren steps into a hidden pocket of beach ringed by rock, the secret cove opening quiet and calm'>>

choices.label: Explore the hidden cove.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=100 beats='the strand line is a museum of the sea, shells, rope, a glass net float, and half buried in the wrack a corked bottle with a curl of paper inside'>>

choices.label: Open the bottle.
Leave it and read the shoreline.
Climb the grassy dunes behind the beach.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='the paper is a message, a mapmaker named Alder writing that the island is real and worth the naming, and that the full chart waits at the ridge camp for whoever finishes the work'>>

choices.label: Set out inland for the ridge camp.
Keep the note as a find and stop here.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=90 beats='Wren tucks Alder's message away and heads back to wait for the ship, carrying a mystery home in place of a map; it is a good story, but a blank chart is still a blank chart, and the apprenticeship will have to wait for another island'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=115 beats='the hidden cove is a perfect pocket of calm inside the reef, and Wren sees at once why Alder loved it, a place no chart has ever shown; a freshwater rill trickles down one wall, a hand cut path climbs toward the ridge, and drawn on a flat rock is an old compass rose pointing up'>>

choices.label: Map the cove, then climb the rill toward the spring.
Follow the compass rose up toward the ridge.
Search the cove's tide pools and sea cave.
Rest too long in the calm cove.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='the cove is so calm that Wren sits to sketch and the morning slips away unnoticed; when the shadows shift Wren startles up with only the cove drawn, one lovely finished corner and a whole blank island around it, and hurries off knowing dusk is closer than it should be'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the path up from the cove is steep and switchbacked, cut long ago by hand, and it climbs past the reach of the sea; halfway up a slab tilts to the sky, pecked all over with dots that are not random'>>

choices.label: Study the dotted slab.
Press straight on up to the ridge.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=95 beats='the slab is a star map, the way the first people found this island by night, dots for the guiding stars and a scratch for the peak beneath them; it is a wonder worth a whole page'>>

choices.label: Copy it quickly and climb on.
Copy it in full and go no further.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=100 beats='Wren fills a page with the star slab and the island shape beneath it, then finds the light has turned and the climb to the ridge is too far now; the chart stays unfinished, but Wren carries home the oldest map of all, the one drawn in stars, and the Chart House keepers gather round it in wonder'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='the cut path tops out on the grassy bench of the main ridge, wind and cloud after the still cove, and the stony camp trail lifts away toward the grey canvas shelter above'>>

choices.label: Climb the camp trail.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=120 beats='the spring is the island's heart, a cold clear pool welling up under ferns where every stream begins; whoever drew the first ochre map marked this place, and from here Wren can finally see how the land fits, water running five ways to five shores, and a clear path climbing on toward the ridge and its camp'>>

choices.label: Fill the flask and climb toward the ridge camp.
Trace the streams to understand the whole island.
Linger mapping every single stream.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='Wren tries to trace every stream from the spring and the task is endless, each rill splitting into more; by the time Wren looks up the sun has swung past noon, and the map is a beautiful tangle of blue lines with no coast to hold them, so Wren heads down with the middle done and the edges bare'>>

<<FILL role=discovery words=100 beats='sitting with the streams, Wren works out the island's whole drainage, every valley and shore linked to this one pool, and pencils it into a clean web that will anchor the finished chart; then the climb to the camp calls'>>

choices.label: Climb to the ridge camp with the water mapped.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the path from the spring climbs out of the ferns onto open ridge where the grass lies flat under the wind, then levels through a high meadow bright with flowers and grazed by a few wild goats'>>

choices.label: Cross the high meadow toward the camp.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='the high meadow is the gentlest ground all day, ringed by an old tumbled boundary cairn a surveyor must have raised; the camp trail lifts away on the far side, but the strange old cairn tugs at Wren's curiosity'>>

choices.label: Cross the meadow to the camp trail.
Investigate the old boundary cairn.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=95 beats='the tumbled cairn holds a flat capstone scratched with a survey mark and an arrow, one of Alder's boundary points fixing the very middle of the island, a small triumph to add to the chart'>>

choices.label: Note the boundary mark and climb to the camp.
Copy the boundary survey in full and rest here.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=95 beats='Wren copies Alder's boundary mark and the way it ties the island together, careful proper work, and then the meadow is so soft and the sun so warm that resting turns to dozing; Wren wakes with a start to a lower sun, the middle of the island mapped and the edges bare, and hurries down with only half a chart'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=125 beats='Alder's ridge camp is a low stone shelter under grey canvas, and stepping in feels like stepping into a story, a cot, a cold stove, and a table where a real chart lies weighted with stones, half the island drawn in a fine sure hand and the rest left for someone to finish; a tin trunk sits closed beneath the cot, and beyond the door the path climbs on toward the summit'>>

choices.label: Study Alder's chart at the table.
Open the tin trunk under the cot.
Take Alder's chart and start for the summit.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=105 beats='Alder's chart is honest and unfinished, the coast and caves drawn true, the high ground and the far shore left blank with a note, finish this and it is yours, mapmaker to mapmaker; the tools to do it lie ready on the table'>>

choices.label: Take up the tools and ready to finish it.
Simply copy Alder's half chart and call it done.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=95 beats='Wren copies Alder's half chart neatly and carries it down to the beach, and it is a fine map, but the blank half is still blank and the hand is still Alder's; the ship's mate frowns at the empty summit and says a copied map is a story, not a survey, so Wren goes home with a tale of the lost mapmaker and no apprenticeship yet'>>

<<FILL role=discovery words=105 beats='the trunk holds Alder's whole island life, fine pens, a spyglass, a signal mirror, and a journal that ends mid sentence the day a storm took the boat; the spyglass and mirror could reach the ship, and the pens could finish any chart'>>

choices.label: Gather the tools and ready to climb on.
Read the journal to its last page.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=100 beats='the journal tells it all, Alder mapping alone, loving the island, naming the cove, and at the end waiting for a ship that came too late; the last line asks whoever finds this to finish the chart and give the island its name'>>

choices.label: Carry Alder's wish on toward the summit.
Close the journal, moved, and simply keep it.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=95 beats='Wren closes Alder's journal with a lump in the throat and decides some things matter more than a test; the afternoon goes to reading every page, and Wren rows out at dusk with a blank chart and a full heart, carrying Alder's story home so the lost mapmaker will not be forgotten'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='tools in hand and Alder's half chart rolled safe, Wren steps back out of the camp with everything needed to finish the work; only the summit remains, the one place high enough to see and draw the whole island at once'>>

choices.label: Set out for the summit.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=105 beats='the path above the camp narrows to a thread of worn grass between two long drops, the summit cairn dark against the racing cloud; a line of cairns marks the safe way, though a bare scree slope offers a tempting shortcut straight up'>>

choices.label: Follow the cairns the safe way.
Try the scree shortcut straight up.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='the scree shortcut slides away the moment Wren's weight goes on it, a slow hissing river of stones, and Wren has to crab sideways off it, breathless, back toward the safe path but shaken and low on time, deciding the summit is a bridge too far today and the high chart stays unfinished'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='the cairns lead through a notch in the ridge where the wind funnels hard enough to lean on, and beyond it the ground opens to the final rise, the summit close now above a last field of tumbled boulders'>>

choices.label: Cross the boulder field to the rise.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='the boulder field is slow careful work, hand and foot from block to block, cloud tearing past, until Wren pulls up over the last of it onto the bare crown of the island with nothing higher left to climb'>>

choices.label: Stand up onto the summit.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=125 beats='the summit is a bare cairn of stacked stones in a river of cloud, and standing on it Wren can see the whole island at last, every shore and reef and secret laid out like a chart already half drawn in the mind; this is the place to finish the work, but the wind is fierce and the light already leans toward afternoon, so every choice from here must count'>>

choices.label: Set up to survey the whole island now.
Signal the far ship first with Alder's mirror.
Just stand and take it all in.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='the view is so vast that Wren simply stares, turning slowly, drinking it in, until a cold gust and a slant of gold light break the spell; there is no time now to draw it properly, only to scramble down, and Wren reaches the beach at dusk with a head full of island and a chart still mostly bare'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=110 beats='from the cairn Wren lays Alder's half chart on a flat stone and begins, sighting the ridge, the reef, the cove, the flats, the shape coming true under the pencil faster than seems possible; but there is more to fix than time allows, so Wren must choose what to nail down first'>>

choices.label: Take careful sightings of every coast.
Fix the reef and cove for safe landings first.
Turn the glass on the wild far shore.
Rush every detail at once.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='trying to draw everything at once, Wren smudges lines together and loses track of which bearing is which, and the page becomes a crowded muddle; there is no time to redraw it, so Wren carries down a chart that is full but wrong in places, honest effort spoiled by hurry, and the mate marks it a near miss'>>

ending.title: All at Once, and None of It

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='Wren works around the cairn taking clean sightings with the salvaged alidade, each far point fixed and noted, the island tightening into true shape; with the bearings gathered it is time to draw the finished chart properly'>>

choices.label: Gather the sketches to draw the final chart.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=105 beats='Wren fixes the reef and cove first, the safe water where a ship can come and go, drawing the channels Alder never lived to finish; it is the most useful map on the island, and with it done there is still a sliver of light to push the whole chart further'>>

choices.label: Push on toward the full masterwork.
Stop with the safe-landing chart done.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=105 beats='Wren rows out with a chart that shows exactly how to enter the island safely, the reef, the cove, the tides, all true; it is not the whole island, but the mate runs a thumb along the clean channels and says this is the map that keeps sailors alive, and takes Wren on with a nod, the masterwork left for next season'>>

ending.title: The Chart That Brings Ships Home

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='sheltered from the wind below the cairn, Wren spreads Alder's chart and the whole day's sketches together, and the island comes whole, coast and cave and ridge and reef fitting like a puzzle; only the drawing itself remains now'>>

choices.label: Draw the final chart.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=105 beats='everything is ready, and the way the chart is drawn will decide everything; Wren can ink it bold and clean, pencil it soft with every small detail, or copy it twice the way a careful surveyor keeps a spare'>>

choices.label: Ink the final chart in bold clean lines.
Pencil it soft and add every small detail.
Copy it twice, one to keep and one to give.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=105 beats='Wren inks the chart with Alder's good pen, every coastline sure and dark, the compass rose crisp, the cove and reef named at last; it is bold and true and unmistakably Wren's own hand finishing Alder's island'>>

choices.label: Sign the finished masterwork.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=105 beats='Wren pencils the chart soft and fine, every tide pool and orchid and handprint cave marked, a map so full it is almost a portrait of the island; it takes longer, and the light is nearly gone, but nothing is left out'>>

choices.label: Sign the detailed masterwork.
Fuss over one last detail as the sun sinks.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='Wren cannot stop adding to the soft pencil map, one more cove, one more current, until the sun is a red rim on the sea and the ship is sounding its horn; Wren runs for the beach with a wonderful unfinished thing, too detailed to call done, and the mate sighs that a map you never finish is no map at all'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=105 beats='Wren copies the chart twice by the failing light, one clean master to give the Chart House and one working copy to keep, the way real surveyors do; it is quick sure work, Alder's island doubled and safe from any single storm'>>

choices.label: Sign both charts.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=105 beats='the masterwork is done, and there is one thing left, the name; Alder's journal asked that whoever finished the chart should name the island, and the blank banner across the top waits for a word'>>

choices.label: Name it for Alder who came before.
Name it for both mapmakers together.
Ring the margins with the island's living things.
Leave the banner blank and sign the survey.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=115 beats='Wren writes Alder's Island across the top in careful letters and rows out as the first stars come, laying the finished masterwork in the mate's hands; the mate reads the name, understands the whole story in it, and says the Chart House has its new apprentice, and that Alder would be proud the island finally has a map and a name'>>

<<FILL role=resolution words=115 beats='Wren writes the names of both mapmakers along the banner, the one who began and the one who finished, and hands the masterwork across as the light fails; the mate studies the perfect chart and the two names and says this is the finest first survey the House has seen in years, and Wren is an apprentice no longer but a mapmaker, name earned and island named'>>

<<FILL role=resolution words=110 beats='Wren leaves the banner blank, signs the survey in the corner as Alder always did, and delivers a chart so complete and true that the mate cannot find a single fault; the name can wait for the House to choose, the mate says, but the place at the Chart House cannot, and offers it to Wren on the spot for the best survey a first islander has ever brought back'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='with two finished charts in hand Wren must decide how to present them to the mate waiting at the boat, both together as proof of a careful surveyor, or one given and one kept as a quiet spare'>>

choices.label: Present both to show a surveyor's care.
Give one and keep the spare quietly.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=115 beats='Wren hands the mate both charts, master and working copy, exactly as a seasoned surveyor would, and explains that a map is too precious to trust to one sheet; the mate turns them over, plainly impressed that a first islander thought like a professional, and gives Wren the apprenticeship for judgement as much as skill, Alder's island saved twice over'>>

<<FILL role=resolution words=105 beats='Wren gives the mate one clean chart and keeps the spare tucked away, and the given map is fine enough to earn a place at the Chart House; the kept copy stays Wren's own, the first of a mapmaker's private library, Alder's island doubled and one half quietly, proudly Wren's'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=105 beats='the ship is a far white speck standing off the reef, and with Alder's signal mirror Wren can throw the sun straight at it; a flash could call the boat in early to the sheltered cove, in time to help the stranded creatures Wren marked in the caves and the flats'>>

choices.label: Aim the mirror and flash the ship.
Signal, then turn back to finish the chart.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='Wren steadies the little mirror against the wind and finds the angle, catching the low sun and throwing a bright blade of light out across the water toward the distant sail'>>

choices.label: Hold the flash steady and wait.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='flash, pause, flash, the old distress rhythm from the Chart House drills, and for a long while nothing, only the wind and the far white sail, until Wren's arm aches and doubt creeps in'>>

choices.label: Keep signalling.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='at last the sail shifts, swinging square, then heeling as the ship comes about and turns its bow toward the island, answering, steering for the sheltered cove; now Wren must get down to meet it in time'>>

choices.label: Race down toward the cove to meet the boat.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='Wren pockets the mirror and goes down the ridge at a controlled run, cairn to cairn, past the camp, down the cut cove path, lungs burning, as the ship's little boat pulls in through the reef below'>>

choices.label: Meet the boat at the cove.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='the boat grounds on the cove sand and two strong sailors jump out, sent by the mate who saw the signal; there is just time before the tide to help one of the stranded creatures Wren mapped, the seal pup in the warm cave or the turtle out on the reef'>>

choices.label: Lead them to the seal pup in the cave.
Lead them to the turtle on the reef.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=115 beats='Wren guides the two sailors to the warm cave and together they lift the seal pup out through the sea tube on the rising tide, and it slips away whole into the green water; the mate watches Wren work and says any mapmaker who reads a coast well enough to save a life is worth keeping, and Wren rows home an apprentice at last, with a good chart and a better story'>>

ending.title: The Pup and the Rising Tide

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=115 beats='Wren leads the sailors across the flats to the reef and they carry the stranded turtle to deep water, where it kicks once and is gone; the sailors cheer, the mate nods slowly, and though the chart is only half finished the mate says a person who uses a map to help rather than only to win has the makings of a true mapmaker, and offers Wren the place'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=105 beats='the still green lagoon is a mirror roofed by mangroves, so quiet the plop of a fish sounds loud; a half sunk dugout canoe lies against the roots, a tunnel of arching roots leads deeper, and a great heap of driftwood dams the far end'>>

choices.label: Bail out the sunken canoe.
Take the root tunnel deeper.
Wade to the driftwood dam.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='the dugout is old but sound, carved by hand long ago, and half full of leaves and rain; bailed out it might carry Wren fast across the lagoon, or it might just as easily fold and sink under a first stroke'>>

choices.label: Bail it and paddle across.
Trust it too far, too fast.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='the old dugout takes water the moment Wren digs in a paddle, and with a slow gurgle it settles until Wren is sitting waist deep in the warm lagoon, sketchbook rescued but the morning wasted wading back to shore, that corner of the coast left a wet blank'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='bailed and balanced, the dugout glides sweetly across the lagoon, and from the water Wren can trace the true shape of the shore far better than from the tangled bank; the far side shows a strip of old cleared ground'>>

choices.label: Land and explore the cleared ground.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='the cleared strip on the far bank is too neat to be wild, someone shaped it once; a low mound of stones and posts stands among the trees, and beyond it a firm path leads back up toward the creek and higher ground'>>

choices.label: Examine the stone mound and posts.
Take the firm path up toward the creek.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=100 beats='the mound is a fish drying rack and the posts a house frame, a whole vanished fishing camp, older than Alder, older than any chart; people lived on this island once and left only this quiet clearing'>>

choices.label: Record the vanished village and climb on.
Sit and map the whole village in loving detail.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=100 beats='Wren maps the vanished fishing camp post by post, imagining the people who dried their catch here, and it fills page after page; it is careful true work, but it is one small clearing, and by the time Wren looks up the island as a whole is still barely begun, so home goes a village and not a chart'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='the root tunnel is a green throat that Wren must duck and wade through, cool and dim, loud with dripping; it bends and forks, one way brightening toward open sky, one way boring deeper into the dark under the island's edge'>>

choices.label: Head for the brightening sky.
Push deeper into the dark.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='the deeper the tunnel goes the more it branches, root passage into root passage, until Wren cannot say which way is out and has to backtrack by feel, heart thudding, to the last patch of light; the lagoon is left a scribble, and Wren climbs out shaken to try open ground'>>

<<FILL role=discovery words=100 beats='the driftwood dam is otter work, years of it, and jammed among the branches is a weathered survey stake with a faded number, one of Alder's marks, still standing where the map said it would be'>>

choices.label: Note Alder's stake and follow the creek.
Prise the stake free as a keepsake.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=95 beats='Wren works Alder's old survey stake loose and turns it over, reading the faded number, and something about holding the real thing makes the whole day feel like Alder's more than Wren's; Wren carries the stake home like a relic, the map itself barely started, a token of the mapmaker who came first'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=105 beats='the roar leads Wren to a waterfall pouring white off a mossy lip into a green pool loud with kingfishers; a wet rock scramble climbs beside it, a mossy log crosses above the lip, and the pool below hides pale shapes under the churn'>>

choices.label: Climb the wet rocks beside the falls.
Cross the mossy log above the lip.
Wade into the pool below.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='the rocks beside the falls are slick with spray and furred with moss, good holds under the green if Wren is careful, a fast way up to the traverse if Wren is bold, and a long wet slide down if Wren is neither'>>

choices.label: Climb carefully, testing each hold.
Scramble up fast and reckless.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='a mossy hold peels away and Wren slithers down the wet rock into the pool in a rush of cold and bubbles, unhurt but soaked to the bone and shivering; the sketchbook is saved held high, but the climbing is over for now, and the high ridge stays unmapped above'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='the mossy log above the lip is broad and, tested, surprisingly firm, and edging across it Wren looks straight down the falling water to the pool far below, then up to where the traverse contours away toward the upper ridge'>>

choices.label: Step off onto the upper traverse.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=100 beats='the pale shapes in the pool are a sunken stone, cut flat and pecked with a scale of little notches, a water gauge someone set to measure the falls through the seasons, Alder's careful hand at work again'>>

choices.label: Note the gauge and climb the falls.
Copy the gauge stone in full detail.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=95 beats='Wren copies Alder's water gauge notch by notch, working out how the falls rise and fall across a year, and it is real science, the kind that makes a place understood; but it eats the hours, and Wren climbs out with a page on the falls and an island still mostly blank, carrying home a piece of Alder's patient study'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=105 beats='a bright hole of daylight hangs high in the cavern roof where a shaft has broken through, and beneath it a slope of fallen rock climbs toward the light; off to one side a gallery of stone formations glows softly, hung with drapes and columns of pale rock'>>

choices.label: Climb the rock slope toward the light.
Explore the gallery of formations.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='the last stretch to the daylight is a narrow chimney of rock that Wren must climb with back against one wall and boots against the other, a hard bright squeeze that would pop Wren out onto the open hillside, high and quick, if the arms hold'>>

choices.label: Chimney up steadily to the surface.
Rush the chimney and tire too soon.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='halfway up the chimney Wren's arms begin to shake and the sensible thing, the only thing, is to slide carefully back down to the cavern floor before a slip becomes a fall; the surface stays out of reach by this route, and Wren must find another way up, time bleeding away'>>

<<FILL role=discovery words=100 beats='the gallery is a frozen forest of stone, columns where drips have met over ages, and among them the small bones of a goat that fell long ago and, beside it, a rusted lantern, proof that someone came searching here before'>>

choices.label: Follow where the searcher went, toward the glitter.
Sketch the whole stone gallery.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=95 beats='Wren draws the stone gallery until the page glows with columns and drapes, and sets the rusted lantern in the corner of the sketch like a signature from the past; it is beautiful, and it is one dark room, and the sunlit island above stays a rumour, so Wren carries home a cave and not a coast'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=105 beats='the keeper's side quarters are small and sad and human, a narrow bunk, a cold hearth, a sea chest at the foot of the bed, and a whole wall pinned with charts gone soft and curling at the edges'>>

choices.label: Read the wall of pinned charts.
Open the sea chest at the bunk's foot.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=100 beats='the pinned charts are not this island at all but a dozen others, coasts and reefs and bays from all across the sea, a whole life of mapmaking, Alder's life, each one signed in the same small hand in the corner'>>

choices.label: Take heart from Alder's life and climb the stair.
Study the wall of a mapmaker's whole life.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=100 beats='Wren stands a long time before Alder's wall of charts, reading a lifetime of islands, and copies a few of the finest into the sketchbook to carry home; it is an education worth more than any test, but the sun does not wait for wonder, and Wren rows out with Alder's story and this one island still blank'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='the sea chest holds a folded oilskin coat, a brass pocket compass that still swings true, and a child's crayon drawing of a house and three small figures, the family Alder left ashore to come and map the world'>>

choices.label: Take the working compass and climb on.
Sit with the child's drawing, undone.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=95 beats='the crayon drawing, a house and three little people waiting, undoes Wren completely, and the morning goes to imagining the family who never saw Alder come home; Wren tucks the drawing carefully away to return it one day, and rows out at dusk with a heart full of Alder's story and a chart still bare'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=105 beats='the mussel beds are a black glistening field that crunches underfoot, sharp enough to mind your step, and busy with oystercatchers; beyond them a colony of seals hauls out on a low rock, and a deep gully cuts a dark line straight through toward the cliffs'>>

choices.label: Approach the basking seals.
Follow the deep gully toward the cliffs.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=100 beats='the seals watch Wren with huge dark eyes and do not flee, a whole colony, mothers and pups, hauled up to rest on the warm rock; among them one small pup lies apart, tangled in a loop of old net and struggling'>>

choices.label: Mark the tangled pup to send help later.
Try to free the tangled pup yourself now.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=100 beats='Wren creeps close and works the old net gently off the pup's flipper until it wriggles free and humps away to its mother, but the colony spooks and floods off the rock, and the fright and the fuss have eaten the tide's low window; a good deed done, the flats left half drawn, Wren wades back proud and behind'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='the deep gully is a shortcut straight to the cliffs, its floor still just dry, but the walls are streaked with weed to well above Wren's head, a plain warning of how deep and how fast it floods when the sea turns'>>

choices.label: Hurry through the gully to the cliff gap.
Dawdle reading the weed lines too long.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='Wren lingers too long puzzling over the weed lines, and the first cold tongue of returning water slides up the gully floor; there is nothing to do but splash back the way in, fast, and take the long safe road, the shortcut lost and a good stretch of the flats left unmapped with the day slipping on'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='Wren turns the alidade on the far shore last of all, the wild windward coast no boat ever lands on, and sketches its cliffs and stacks and the white line of its reef, ground that has never been on any chart at all'>>

choices.label: Add the far shore and gather the whole survey.
Stop, thrilled, having charted the unknown coast.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=105 beats='Wren carries down a chart of the wild windward shore alone, the one coast no mapmaker ever reached, and though the rest of the island is only sketched the mate whistles at the sight of that unknown coast drawn true; it is not the masterwork, but it is real new ground, and the mate says a surveyor who finds the unfindable is worth training, and keeps Wren on'>>

ending.title: The Coast No One Had Mapped

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='with the masterwork inked and signed, the little boat waiting, Wren has one last choice as the light goes gold, hand the chart over now and be sure of it, or add the two rescued creatures and the vanished village to the margin as living proof the island is more than lines'>>

choices.label: Add the living island to the margins.
Hand over the clean masterwork as it is.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=115 beats='Wren rings the finished chart with tiny true drawings, the seal pup, the turtle, the vanished fishing camp, the star slab, so the map holds not just the shape of the island but its life; the mate turns it in the last light and says this is more than a survey, it is the island itself on paper, and gives Wren the apprenticeship and the honour of naming the next island too'>>

<<FILL role=resolution words=110 beats='Wren hands over the clean inked masterwork exactly as drawn, coast and reef and ridge all true, no flourish, no fuss, and the mate checks it corner to corner and finds it faultless; a clean honest survey, the mate says, is what the Chart House lives on, and Wren is taken on as the newest apprentice mapmaker on the strength of a perfect first island'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=105 beats='Wren poles a raft of lashed reeds across to the heron island, a low green mound white with nesting birds and loud as a market; a weathered watching blind stands on stilts, a wide mud bank shelves off one side, and the treetops seethe with a whole rookery'>>

choices.label: Climb into the old watching blind.
Wade out onto the wide mud bank.
Count the nests in the rookery.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=100 beats='the blind is a surveyor's hide, and Alder used it well, the rails scratched with a whole tide table and the compass bearings of every headland in sight, a place to sit still and let the island give up its shape'>>

choices.label: Copy the bearings and take the plank walk inland.
Copy the entire carved tide table in full.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=95 beats='Wren copies Alder's whole carved tide table, hour by hour, a treasure of exactly when each flat floods, and it fills the morning; it will make some future chart wonderful, but today it is one rail of scratched numbers, and the island stays mostly unmapped as Wren poles reluctantly back'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='the mud bank is soft and shining and crowded with wading birds, and a firm ribbon of sand threads across it toward a far channel and higher ground, but step wrong off the ribbon and the mud is knee deep and grasping'>>

choices.label: Follow the firm ribbon to the far channel.
Cut straight across and startle the flock.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='Wren cuts across the soft mud and a thousand birds explode up at once in a storm of wings and noise that sends Wren stumbling and floundering knee deep; by the time the flock settles and Wren struggles clear, plastered and laughing and shaken, the tide is turning and the heron island is left a hasty smudge'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='the firm ribbon delivers Wren dry shod to the far channel, where the water runs clear toward the mainland shore and the ground beyond lifts, green and solid, toward the northern lookout rise'>>

choices.label: Cross to the lookout rise.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=95 beats='counting the nests, Wren finds one fallen chick cheeping in the grass and lifts it gently back to a low branch under its parents' cross eyed glare, then sketches the whole seething rookery for the map's margin'>>

choices.label: Sketch the rookery and cross to the mud bank.
Stay to watch the whole colony too long.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=95 beats='Wren cannot stop watching the herons, the fishing, the squabbles, the fallen chick settling safe, and the sketchbook fills with birds instead of coastline; it is a lovely living page, but the sun climbs on unmapped, and Wren poles back with a rookery drawn and an island still waiting'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=105 beats='the grove is a cathedral of giant moss hung trees, the light green and holy, the air still after the ridge wind; a frayed rope ladder climbs into the high canopy, and deep among the roots stands a small cairn shrine dressed with shells'>>

choices.label: Climb the old rope ladder into the canopy.
Kneel to the little cairn shrine.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='the rope ladder is grey and frayed but each rung holds when Wren tests it, and above the leaves must lie a view over the whole ridge; still, the higher rungs look thinner, and trusting them fully is a gamble'>>

choices.label: Climb steadily, testing every rung.
Trust the frayed upper rungs and hurry.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='a high rung parts with a soft rotten crack and Wren drops a heart stopping foot before the next rung catches, and that is enough, down is the only sensible way now, careful hand under careful hand; the canopy view is lost, and the grove goes on the map as a green guess from the ground'>>

<<FILL role=discovery words=100 beats='the ladder tops out on a plank platform lashed high in the crown, Alder's treetop survey point, a fixed brass sighting ring still bolted to the trunk, and through the leaves the whole green ridge rolls away to the sea'>>

choices.label: Take bearings from the platform and climb down to the trail.
Linger in the swaying green crown too long.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=95 beats='the swaying green world of the canopy is so wonderful that Wren stays and stays, sketching the ridge through gaps in the leaves, until the light tells the truth about the hour; it is a rare view few ever see, but it is one treetop, and Wren climbs down with a beautiful fragment and no finished chart'>>

<<FILL role=discovery words=95 beats='the shrine is a knee high cairn dressed with shells and a single carved wooden bird, weathered soft, older than Alder, a quiet marker left by the island's first people to bless the high forest'>>

choices.label: Record the shrine and climb on toward the camp.
Sit long in the green hush before the shrine.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=95 beats='Wren sits a long peaceful while before the little shrine, drawing the shell dressed cairn and the carved bird, feeling the weight of all the people who loved this island before; the grove empties Wren of hurry, which is lovely and costly both, and the day slips by with only the shrine set down'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=105 beats='the passage opens on an underground lake, black and perfectly still, its far shore lost in dark; a raft of old lashed planks is drawn up on a beach of pale sand, and the ceiling glitters faintly where water has carried the crystal down'>>

choices.label: Pole the old raft across the black lake.
Search the pale sand beach.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='the raft floats when Wren eases it out, and a long pole finds the bottom, so crossing is possible, slow and eerie over the black glass water; but the lashings are old, and to hurry or lean is to risk a cold dark swim'>>

choices.label: Pole across slow and steady.
Push off hard and pole in haste.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='a lashing gives with a wet snap and the raft tips, and Wren is suddenly waist deep in the cold black lake, gasping, hauling the raft and the precious dry sketchbook back to the pale beach by feel; there is no crossing now, and the far dark of the cave keeps its secrets while Wren wrings out and retreats'>>

<<FILL role=discovery words=100 beats='the far shore is a wonder, blind white fish hanging in the shallows and a natural stair of flowstone climbing toward a distant seam of grey daylight high in the rock'>>

choices.label: Climb the flowstone stair toward the daylight.
Stay to study the blind white fish.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=95 beats='Wren watches the blind white fish drift in water that has never seen the sun and fills a page with creatures no chart ever showed, patient wondering work; it is a discovery to make the Chart House gasp, but it is a single dark pool, and the sunlit island above waits unmapped as the hours drain away'>>

<<FILL role=discovery words=95 beats='the pale sand holds prints, and stooping close Wren sees they are turned to stone, the bare footprints of children who walked this hidden beach an age ago and left their steps behind them in the deep'>>

choices.label: Record the stone footprints and climb out to the ridge.
Map every ancient footprint on the beach.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=95 beats='Wren maps every stone footprint on the hidden beach, imagining the children who made them in the dark long ago, and the wonder of it holds Wren fast; page after page fills with little vanished steps, and when Wren finally looks up the day is old and the island above is still nearly blank'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=105 beats='on a jut of the headland stands the old signal beacon, a great rusted iron brazier on a stone base with a coil of chain and, set into the rock beside it, a carved stone dial marking bearings out to every landmark on the coast'>>

choices.label: Inspect the rusted brazier.
Read the carved bearing dial.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='the brazier is cold and empty, its last fire long dead, but Wren sees how it worked, how a blaze here would warn ships off the reef; there must have been a fuel store nearby, and finding it would be a fine thing to mark, though it will cost time'>>

choices.label: Search along the headland for the fuel store.
Give up on the dead beacon and turn back.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='Wren pokes about the cold brazier and finds nothing but rust and old ash, no way to light it and precious little to map, and the search has swallowed a good hour for a dead end; a little cross with the wasted time, Wren turns back to the ridge with the beacon barely worth a dot on the paper'>>

<<FILL role=discovery words=100 beats='tucked under a stone lintel Wren finds the fuel store, empty now, its door scrawled inside with a keeper's tally and a last line in Alder's hand, gone to finish the ridge chart, back by the storm, a message left for a ship that never came'>>

choices.label: Copy Alder's last message and cross to the ridge.
Sit with Alder's unanswered message.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=95 beats='Alder's scrawled line, back by the storm, undoes Wren a little, and the morning goes to sitting with it, copying it, wondering about the storm that changed everything; it is a piece of the lost mapmaker's story worth carrying home, but a story is not a survey, and the chart stays as empty as the fuel store'>>

<<FILL role=discovery words=95 beats='the carved dial is a gift, a stone compass rose with a groove and a name cut for every headland, reef, and peak in sight, Alder fixing the whole coast in stone so no fog could ever wholly hide it'>>

choices.label: Copy the dial's bearings and cross to the ridge.
Copy every single bearing on the great dial.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=95 beats='Wren copies every bearing cut into Alder's stone dial, a perfect skeleton for any chart, and it is genuinely useful work, the bones of a survey handed down; but copying it all eats the light, and Wren carries home Alder's bearings with no coastline drawn between them, a frame around an empty island'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=105 beats='the wreck lies keeled over on the bar, a great old ship gone to ribs like the bones of a whale, timbers black with age; the flooded hold gapes below, the tilted deck climbs above, and at the bow a carved figurehead still leans out over the sand'>>

choices.label: Reach down into the flooded hold.
Climb the tilted deck to the bow.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='the hold is half full of green water and dim shapes of old cargo, reachable if Wren wades in now, but the light is turning silver out on the flats, the tide's first warning, and a person caught in a wreck by the sea is a person in real trouble'>>

choices.label: Wade in quickly for a look at the cargo.
Push your luck deep in the flooding hold.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='Wren wades too deep into the wreck just as the tide slides back across the bar, and for one cold heart lurching moment the way out is a narrowing gap of grey water; Wren scrambles free soaked and scared, the lesson learned hard, and splashes to shore with the wreck half seen and the flats left dangerously unfinished'>>

<<FILL role=discovery words=100 beats='in the hold Wren finds barrels furred with weed and, wedged in the ribs, a great green brass bell with a name still raised on it, the Marigold, the very ship that carried the island's first settlers and never sailed home'>>

choices.label: Note the settler ship and wade on to the cliff gap.
Stay to read the whole story of the wreck.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=95 beats='Wren pieces together the wreck's whole story from the bell and the barrels and the ribs, the Marigold that brought the first families and foundered on the bar, and it is a tale to make the Chart House hush; but the telling costs the tide, and Wren wades back with a settler ship remembered and the flats left a soaking blur'>>

<<FILL role=discovery words=95 beats='the tilted deck climbs to the bow where the figurehead leans, a carved woman with a name board beneath, and from the high stern rail Wren can see the whole pattern of the flats and channels laid out clear as a drawn map below'>>

choices.label: Sketch the flats from the high stern and climb down to the cliff gap.
Stay on the bow to draw the figurehead in loving detail.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=95 beats='Wren perches at the bow and draws the weathered figurehead until she seems almost to breathe, the carved guardian of a ship that never came home, and it is a haunting lovely page; but the flats do not care for art, and the returning tide chases Wren down with the figurehead finished and the coast still bare'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=105 beats='the cove's rim is a garden of tide pools, each a jewelled world of anemone and darting fish, and low in the cliff a small sea cave breathes with the swell; the pools would map the reef edge beautifully, and the cave murmurs of something further in'>>

choices.label: Explore the low sea cave.
Study the jewelled tide pools.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=100 beats='the sea cave is shallow and dry at its back, and there, cut into the rock, are Alder's initials and a date, and wedged in a cleft a waterproof tin holding a spare pencil and a folded scrap that reads, the way up is behind you, friend'>>

choices.label: Take Alder's pencil and climb the cove path.
Sit with Alder's kind hidden note.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=95 beats='the little note, the way up is behind you, friend, feels like Alder reaching a hand across the years, and Wren sits with it a long grateful while in the breathing cave; it is a moment worth more than any chart, but the moment stretches, and Wren climbs out at last with Alder's pencil, a full heart, and an empty page'>>

<<FILL role=discovery words=95 beats='the tide pools are the reef's edge in miniature, and mapping them Wren works out the whole run of the cove's channel, the safe deep line a boat could follow in, drawing what Alder began; then the cove path calls upward'>>

choices.label: Add the reef edge and climb toward the ridge.
Map every last pool in jewelled detail.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=95 beats='Wren maps every tide pool in loving jewelled detail, anemone by anemone, and the cove's reef edge comes out perfect on the page, genuinely useful for any boat; but the perfect corner costs the day, and Wren climbs away at last with one flawless reef and a whole blank island above it'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the side rift is hung with glowworms like a fallen sky, thousands of tiny blue green lights in the black; the rift narrows to a squeeze at one end, and at the other a still pool catches the glow and doubles it'>>

choices.label: Photograph the glowworms in memory and squeeze through.
Sit beneath the glowing ceiling.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=90 beats='the squeeze is a hand's breadth too narrow to be comfortable, cold rock pressing close, but a wriggle of daring would pop Wren out into the wider cave beyond, or wedge Wren fast for a frightening minute'>>

choices.label: Wriggle carefully through the squeeze.
Force it in a panicky rush.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='Wren pushes too hard too fast and sticks fast in the cold squeeze for one long horrible minute, breathing slow, before wriggling backward free; that is quite enough of narrow places, and Wren retreats the way in with the rift left as a shaky note and a firm resolve to keep to wider caves'>>

<<FILL role=discovery words=95 beats='under the glowing ceiling Wren draws the rift by its own blue light, the worms, the doubled pool, a page like a piece of night sky brought underground, and finds an easy way on toward the sound of the spring'>>

choices.label: Follow the easy way to the spring.
Stay lost in the glowing dark too long.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=95 beats='the glowworm rift is so beautiful that Wren lies back and simply watches the living stars, and time comes unstuck the way it does under the ground; when Wren finally stirs the day above has moved on, and home goes a page of underground sky and an island still waiting for its map'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the deer path opens on a hidden dell, soft grass and a ring of ferns around a trickling spring, so sheltered and green it feels like a secret room; a mossy carved bench sits by the water, and a faint way climbs on toward the upper ridge'>>

choices.label: Follow the dell spring downhill.
Examine the mossy carved bench.
Doze in the secret green room.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='the dell is so peaceful that Wren means to rest a moment and instead rests an hour, and wakes with a jolt to a higher sun and a colder shadow; the secret green room has quietly stolen the morning, and Wren hurries out with the dell drawn and far too much of the island still blank'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='the dell spring gathers into a proper stream and leads Wren gently down through the ferns to the wider cold pool of the island's true spring, the whole watershed suddenly making sense'>>

choices.label: Come out at the spring.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=95 beats='the bench is carved with initials and little maps by mapmakers who rested here across the years, Alder's among them, a guest book in wood; adding a mark of your own feels like joining a long quiet company'>>

choices.label: Add your mark and climb to the upper ridge.
Read every name carved in the old bench.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=95 beats='Wren reads every name and little map carved into the old bench, a history of mapmakers resting here going back before Alder, and copies them all down, moved; it is a lovely record of the ones who came before, but it is not a chart, and the day slides on with the dell drawn and the island bare'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='the loose slab lifts on a cavity in the floor, and in it sits a small iron strongbox, cold and heavy and locked; a rusted key hangs on a nail nearby, but the lock is seized, and forcing it might cost a bent tool or a barked knuckle'>>

choices.label: Try the rusted key patiently.
Lever the seized lock by main force.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='Wren levers at the seized lock until the makeshift tool bends and slips and rakes a knuckle, and the strongbox stays stubbornly shut; nursing the graze, Wren gives it up as a bad job and a lost half hour, and leaves the tower's last secret locked for someone with the proper key'>>

<<FILL role=discovery words=100 beats='the rusted key turns at last, and the strongbox opens on a mapmaker's treasure, a set of fine brass drawing tools and a sealed letter addressed simply, to whoever finishes my work, in Alder's unmistakable hand'>>

choices.label: Take the tools and the letter and climb the stair.
Open and read Alder's sealed letter now.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=100 beats='Wren breaks the seal and reads Alder's letter to the one who would come after, a gentle charge to love the island and finish the map and never mind the glory, and it lands like a hand on the shoulder; the reading takes what is left of the good light, and Wren rows out with Alder's blessing, the brass tools, and a chart still to draw'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='the grassy dunes roll behind the beach, whispering with marram grass, and from their crest Wren could see the whole sweep of the flats laid out clear; a weathered marker post leans on the highest dune, but the soft sand is exhausting to climb and the tide waits for no one'>>

choices.label: Climb to the marker post for the view.
Wallow too long in the soft dunes.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='the soft sand slides away under every step and the climb turns into a floundering slog that leaves Wren sweating and no higher, and the tide is already whispering back across the flats below; Wren gives up the dune and trudges down, the great view unwon and the flats left a hurried scribble'>>

<<FILL role=discovery words=100 beats='the marker post is a surveyor's, notched and painted long ago, and from its dune Wren can see the entire pattern of the flats, the channels, the bars, the safe firm roads across, and sketches it all in one clear commanding view'>>

choices.label: Take the whole flats in one view and follow the channels in.
Stay on the breezy dune drawing every channel.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=95 beats='from the marker dune Wren draws the flats entire, every channel and bar and safe crossing, a genuinely fine map of the shore; but the commanding view is so satisfying that Wren stays too long perfecting it, and climbs down at last with the flats beautifully done and the rest of the island untouched'>>
